Across those sectors, adoption is furthest along in lower-risk operational and productivity use cases such as clinical documentation, fraud detection, classroom support, public-service workflow automation, and research tooling, while high-stakes or frontline decisions remain more cautious. New Zealand is moving past experimentation, but progress is still uneven and constrained by governance, trust, skills, privacy, infrastructure, and the challenge of scaling pilots into durable capability.

This living whitepaper currently tracks AI in academic research, agriculture, creative industries, education, finance, healthcare, and the public sector in Aotearoa New Zealand.

Updated 09 March 2026
